message.content: how do I run the demo?

content.text:
From the `web/` directory:

```bash
pnpm demo              # auto-advance with 3s pause
pnpm demo:manual       # click "Next Step ›" to advance
pnpm demo:test         # headless smoke test
```

The demo is a Playwright-driven browser walkthrough that lives in `web/demo/`. You'll need the dev server running first (`make dev` from the project root, or `pnpm dev` from `web/`).

## Kapow Monorepo Makefile — delegates to component Makefiles
## Usage: make <target>

.PHONY: api-build api-run api-generate api-test api-test-unit \
        api-lint api-migrate-up api-migrate-down api-clean schema-gen \
        web-install web-dev web-build web-lint web-clean web-test web-test-coverage web-e2e web-screenshots web-storybook \
        web-schema-sync \
        lint audit hadolint \
        smoke-test smoke-test-with-server \
        api-image-build web-image-build test-image-build test-image-run \
        tools-build tools-test \
        dev dev-clean dev-prune dev-db-up dev-db-down \
        compose-up compose-down compose-logs \
        test-quick test-standard test-full test-fixture \
        fixture-sql fixture-json \
        build-full \
        spec-gen \
        help

## api-build: Build the API server binary
api-build:
	$(MAKE) -C api build

## build-full: Build single binary with embedded web frontend (API + SPA)
build-full: web-build
	$(MAKE) -C api build-full

## api-run: Run the API server
api-run:
	$(MAKE) -C api run

## api-generate: Run GraphQL code generation
api-generate:
	$(MAKE) -C api generate

## api-test: Run all API tests
api-test:
	$(MAKE) -C api test

## api-test-unit: Run API unit tests
api-test-unit:
	$(MAKE) -C api test-unit

## api-lint: Run linter on the API
api-lint:
	$(MAKE) -C api lint

## api-migrate-up: Apply database migrations
api-migrate-up:
	$(MAKE) -C api migrate-up

## api-migrate-down: Roll back last database migration
api-migrate-down:
	$(MAKE) -C api migrate-down

## api-clean: Remove API build artifacts
api-clean:
	$(MAKE) -C api clean

## schema-gen: Generate common/schema/schema.sql from migration Up blocks
schema-gen:
	bash scripts/gen-schema.sh

## web-install: Install web frontend dependencies
web-install:
	$(MAKE) -C web install

## web-dev: Start Vite dev server (port 8080, proxies /graphql → :8081)
web-dev:
	$(MAKE) -C web dev

## web-build: Type-check and build web frontend for production
web-build:
	$(MAKE) -C web build

## web-lint: Run ESLint on web frontend
web-lint:
	$(MAKE) -C web lint

## web-clean: Remove web build artifacts and node_modules
web-clean:
	$(MAKE) -C web clean

## web-test: Run web unit tests
web-test:
	$(MAKE) -C web test

## web-test-coverage: Run web unit tests with coverage report
web-test-coverage:
	$(MAKE) -C web test-coverage

## web-e2e: Run Playwright E2E tests for web frontend (starts Vite dev server automatically)
web-e2e:
	$(MAKE) -C web e2e-test

## web-screenshots: Capture visual baseline screenshots across viewports
web-screenshots:
	$(MAKE) -C web screenshots

## web-storybook: Start Storybook dev server for UI components (port 6006)
web-storybook:
	$(MAKE) -C web storybook

## web-schema-sync: Regenerate web/schema.graphql and web/src/graphql-env.d.ts from API SDL files
## Run this after any change to api/graph/schema/*.graphql
web-schema-sync:
	cat api/graph/schema/schema.graphql \
	    api/graph/schema/auth.graphql \
	    api/graph/schema/data_sources.graphql \
	    api/graph/schema/echo.graphql \
	    api/graph/schema/field_names_gen.graphql \
	    api/graph/schema/institution.graphql \
	    api/graph/schema/preferences.graphql \
	    api/graph/schema/search.graphql \
	    api/graph/schema/search_fields.graphql \
	    api/graph/schema/similarity.graphql \
	    api/graph/schema/zip.graphql \
	    > web/schema.graphql
	cd web && pnpm exec gql-tada generate-output --tsconfig tsconfig.app.json

## lint: Run all linters (Go + web)
lint: api-lint web-lint

## audit: Run security audit on web dependencies (non-zero exit on high+ severity)
audit:
	cd web && pnpm audit --audit-level=high

## hadolint: Lint all Dockerfiles with hadolint (runs via Docker)
hadolint:
	@find . -name 'Dockerfile*' -not -path '*/node_modules/*' -not -path '*/dist/*' | sort | \
		while read -r f; do \
			echo "Linting $$f"; \
			docker run --rm -i hadolint/hadolint < "$$f" || exit 1; \
		done

## smoke-test: Run search smoke test against localhost:8081 (requires running API)
smoke-test:
	bash scripts/smoke-test.sh

## smoke-test-with-server: Build API, start it, run smoke test, tear down (requires DATABASE_URL)
smoke-test-with-server:
	bash scripts/smoke-test-with-server.sh

## api-image-build: Build the standalone API production Docker image
api-image-build:
	docker build -f api/Dockerfile \
		--build-arg VERSION=$$(git describe --tags --always --dirty 2>/dev/null || echo dev) \
		--build-arg BUILD_TIME=$$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ) \
		-t kapow-api \
		api/

## web-image-build: Build the standalone web frontend Docker image (nginx + Vite build)
web-image-build:
	docker build -t kapow-web web/

## test-image-build: Build the all-in-one test Docker image (postgres+API+nginx)
test-image-build: hadolint
	docker build -t kapow-test .

## test-image-run: Run the test image, exposing the web UI on http://localhost:8080
test-image-run: test-image-build
	docker run --rm -p 8080:80 kapow-test

## dev: Start full-stack dev environment (PostgreSQL + API + web) with hot reload
dev:
	bash scripts/dev.sh

## dev-clean: Remove this checkout's dev database volume (forces fresh fixture load on next `make dev`)
dev-clean:
	@HASH=$$(echo "$$PWD" | md5sum | cut -c1-8); \
	VOL="kapow-dev-pgdata-$$HASH"; \
	CONT="kapow-dev-$$HASH"; \
	docker rm -f "$$CONT" 2>/dev/null || true; \
	if docker volume inspect "$$VOL" >/dev/null 2>&1; then \
		docker volume rm "$$VOL"; \
		echo "Removed volume $$VOL"; \
	else \
		echo "Volume $$VOL does not exist"; \
	fi

## dev-db-up: Start standalone dev database (PostgreSQL+PostGIS on port 5433, full dataset)
dev-db-up:
	docker compose -f compose/db.yml up -d --wait
	@echo ""
	@echo "Dev database starting on port 5433. Use:"
	@echo "  DATABASE_URL=postgres://postgres:postgres@localhost:5433/kapow?sslmode=disable"

## dev-db-down: Stop and remove the standalone dev database container
dev-db-down:
	docker compose -f compose/db.yml down

## compose-up: Start 3-service production topology (db + api + web) on http://localhost:8080
compose-up:
	docker compose -f compose/compose.yml up -d --build --wait

## compose-down: Stop and remove compose services and volumes
compose-down:
	docker compose -f compose/compose.yml down -v

## compose-logs: Tail logs from all compose services
compose-logs:
	docker compose -f compose/compose.yml logs -f

## dev-prune: Remove kapow-dev volumes older than 30 days
dev-prune:
	@CUTOFF=$$(date -d '30 days ago' +%s 2>/dev/null || date -v-30d +%s); \
	for vol in $$(docker volume ls --filter label=kapow-dev=true -q 2>/dev/null); do \
		CREATED=$$(docker volume inspect "$$vol" --format '{{.CreatedAt}}' 2>/dev/null); \
		VOL_TS=$$(date -d "$$CREATED" +%s 2>/dev/null || date -j -f "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S" "$$CREATED" +%s 2>/dev/null || echo 0); \
		if [ "$$VOL_TS" -lt "$$CUTOFF" ]; then \
			echo "Removing old volume: $$vol (created $$CREATED)"; \
			docker volume rm "$$vol" 2>/dev/null || true; \
		fi; \
	done; \
	echo "Prune complete."

## tools-build: Build all tools (samplesql)
tools-build:
	cd tools/samplesql && go build -o ../../bin/samplesql .

## tools-test: Run tests for all tools
tools-test:
	cd tools/samplesql && go test ./...

## test-quick: Fast checks — Go unit tests, TS type-check + build, ESLint (~10-20s, no DB)
test-quick:
	MAKE=$(MAKE) bash scripts/parallel-run.sh api-test-unit web-build web-lint

## test-standard: Full local validation — quick + web unit tests + Go lint (~30-60s, no DB)
test-standard:
	$(MAKE) test-quick && MAKE=$(MAKE) bash scripts/parallel-run.sh web-test api-lint

## test-fixture: Validate fixture data loads cleanly against current schema (spins up temp Docker PG)
test-fixture:
	bash scripts/test-fixture.sh

## test-full: Complete validation — standard + integration, e2e, smoke, fixture (requires DB + running services)
test-full:
	$(MAKE) test-standard && MAKE=$(MAKE) bash scripts/parallel-run.sh api-test test-fixture tools-test && $(MAKE) web-e2e && $(MAKE) smoke-test

## fixture-sql: Generate complete.sql.xz from JSON fixture data (requires Docker)
fixture-sql:
	bash scripts/gen-fixture-sql.sh

## fixture-json: Re-extract JSON fixture data from a live database (requires DATABASE_URL)
fixture-json:
	cd tools/samplesql && go run . --format json --output-dir ../../common/src/main/resources/data/tables

## spec-gen: Generate reference documentation in docs/specs/generated/
spec-gen:
	bash scripts/gen-specs.sh

## help: List all available targets
help:
	@grep -E '^## ' Makefile | sed 's/## //' | column -t -s ':'

	    api/graph/schema/echo.graphql \

	cd web && pnpm exec gql-tada generate-output --tsconfig tsconfig.app.json

## lint: Run all linters (Go + web)
lint: api-lint web-lint

## audit: Run security audit on web dependencies (non-zero exit on high+ severity)
audit:
	cd web && pnpm audit --audit-level=high

## hadolint: Lint all Dockerfiles with hadolint (runs via Docker)
hadolint:
	@find . -name 'Dockerfile*' -not -path '*/node_modules/*' -not -path '*/dist/*' | sort | \
		while read -r f; do \
			echo "Linting $$f"; \
			docker run --rm -i hadolint/hadolint < "$$f" || exit 1; \
		done

## smoke-test: Run search smoke test against localhost:8081 (requires running API)
smoke-test:
	bash scripts/smoke-test.sh

## smoke-test-with-server: Build API, start it, run smoke test, tear down (requires DATABASE_URL)
smoke-test-with-server:
	bash scripts/smoke-test-with-server.sh

## api-image-build: Build the standalone API production Docker image
api-image-build:
	docker build -f api/Dockerfile \
		--build-arg VERSION=$$(git describe --tags --always --dirty 2>/dev/null || echo dev) \
		--build-arg BUILD_TIME=$$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ) \
		-t kapow-api \
		api/

## web-image-build: Build the standalone web frontend Docker image (nginx + Vite build)
web-image-build:
	docker build -t kapow-web web/

## test-image-build: Build the all-in-one test Docker image (postgres+API+nginx)
test-image-build: hadolint
	docker build -t kapow-test .

## test-image-run: Run the test image, exposing the web UI on http://localhost:8080
test-image-run: test-image-build
	docker run --rm -p 8080:80 kapow-test

## dev: Start full-stack dev environment (PostgreSQL + API + web) with hot reload
dev:
	bash scripts/dev.sh

## dev-clean: Remove this checkout's dev database volume (forces fresh fixture load on next `make dev`)
dev-clean:
	@HASH=$$(echo "$$PWD" | md5sum | cut -c1-8); \
	VOL="kapow-dev-pgdata-$$HASH"; \
	CONT="kapow-dev-$$HASH"; \
	docker rm -f "$$CONT" 2>/dev/null || true; \
	if docker volume inspect "$$VOL" >/dev/null 2>&1; then \
		docker volume rm "$$VOL"; \
		echo "Removed volume $$VOL"; \
	else \
		echo "Volume $$VOL does not exist"; \
	fi

## dev-db-up: Start standalone dev database (PostgreSQL+PostGIS on port 5433, full dataset)
dev-db-up:
	docker compose -f compose/db.yml up -d --wait
	@echo ""
	@echo "Dev database starting on port 5433. Use:"
	@echo "  DATABASE_URL=postgres://postgres:postgres@localhost:5433/kapow?sslmode=disable"

## dev-db-down: Stop and remove the standalone dev database container
dev-db-down:
	docker compose -f compose/db.yml down

## compose-up: Start 3-service production topology (db + api + web) on http://localhost:8080
compose-up:
	docker compose -f compose/compose.yml up -d --build --wait

## compose-down: Stop and remove compose services and volumes
compose-down:
	docker compose -f compose/compose.yml down -v

## compose-logs: Tail logs from all compose services
compose-logs:
	docker compose -f compose/compose.yml logs -f

## dev-prune: Remove kapow-dev volumes older than 30 days
dev-prune:
	@CUTOFF=$$(date -d '30 days ago' +%s 2>/dev/null || date -v-30d +%s); \
	for vol in $$(docker volume ls --filter label=kapow-dev=true -q 2>/dev/null); do \
		CREATED=$$(docker volume inspect "$$vol" --format '{{.CreatedAt}}' 2>/dev/null); \
		VOL_TS=$$(date -d "$$CREATED" +%s 2>/dev/null || date -j -f "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S" "$$CREATED" +%s 2>/dev/null || echo 0); \
		if [ "$$VOL_TS" -lt "$$CUTOFF" ]; then \
			echo "Removing old volume: $$vol (created $$CREATED)"; \
			docker volume rm "$$vol" 2>/dev/null || true; \
		fi; \
	done; \
	echo "Prune complete."

## tools-build: Build all tools (samplesql)
tools-build:
	cd tools/samplesql && go build -o ../../bin/samplesql .

## tools-test: Run tests for all tools
tools-test:
	cd tools/samplesql && go test ./...

## test-quick: Fast checks — Go unit tests, TS type-check + build, ESLint (~10-20s, no DB)
test-quick:
	MAKE=$(MAKE) bash scripts/parallel-run.sh api-test-unit web-build web-lint

## test-standard: Full local validation — quick + web unit tests + Go lint (~30-60s, no DB)
test-standard:
	$(MAKE) test-quick && MAKE=$(MAKE) bash scripts/parallel-run.sh web-test api-lint

## test-fixture: Validate fixture data loads cleanly against current schema (spins up temp Docker PG)
test-fixture:
	bash scripts/test-fixture.sh

## test-full: Complete validation — standard + integration, e2e, smoke, fixture (requires DB + running services)
test-full:
	$(MAKE) test-standard && MAKE=$(MAKE) bash scripts/parallel-run.sh api-test test-fixture tools-test && $(MAKE) web-e2e && $(MAKE) smoke-test

## fixture-sql: Generate complete.sql.xz from JSON fixture data (requires Docker)
fixture-sql:
	bash scripts/gen-fixture-sql.sh

## fixture-json: Re-extract JSON fixture data from a live database (requires DATABASE_URL)
fixture-json:
	cd tools/samplesql && go run . --format json --output-dir ../../common/src/main/resources/data/tables

## spec-gen: Generate reference documentation in docs/specs/generated/
spec-gen:
	bash scripts/gen-specs.sh

## help: List all available targets
help:
	@grep -E '^## ' Makefile | sed 's/## //' | column -t -s ':'

	    api/graph/schema/search_fields.graphql \
	    api/graph/schema/similarity.graphql \
	    api/graph/schema/zip.graphql \
	    > web/schema.graphql
	cd web && pnpm exec gql-tada generate-output --tsconfig tsconfig.app.json

## lint: Run all linters (Go + web)
lint: api-lint web-lint

## audit: Run security audit on web dependencies (non-zero exit on high+ severity)
audit:
	cd web && pnpm audit --audit-level=high

## hadolint: Lint all Dockerfiles with hadolint (runs via Docker)
hadolint:
	@find . -name 'Dockerfile*' -not -path '*/node_modules/*' -not -path '*/dist/*' | sort | \
		while read -r f; do \
			echo "Linting $$f"; \
			docker run --rm -i hadolint/hadolint < "$$f" || exit 1; \
		done

## smoke-test: Run search smoke test against localhost:8081 (requires running API)
smoke-test:
	bash scripts/smoke-test.sh

## smoke-test-with-server: Build API, start it, run smoke test, tear down (requires DATABASE_URL)
smoke-test-with-server:
	bash scripts/smoke-test-with-server.sh

## api-image-build: Build the standalone API production Docker image
api-image-build:
	docker build -f api/Dockerfile \
		--build-arg VERSION=$$(git describe --tags --always --dirty 2>/dev/null || echo dev) \
		--build-arg BUILD_TIME=$$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ) \
		-t kapow-api \
		api/

## web-image-build: Build the standalone web frontend Docker image (nginx + Vite build)
web-image-build:
	docker build -t kapow-web web/

## test-image-build: Build the all-in-one test Docker image (postgres+API+nginx)
test-image-build: hadolint
	docker build -t kapow-test .

## test-image-run: Run the test image, exposing the web UI on http://localhost:8080
test-image-run: test-image-build
	docker run --rm -p 8080:80 kapow-test

## dev: Start full-stack dev environment (PostgreSQL + API + web) with hot reload
dev:
	bash scripts/dev.sh

## dev-clean: Remove this checkout's dev database volume (forces fresh fixture load on next `make dev`)
dev-clean:
	@HASH=$$(echo "$$PWD" | md5sum | cut -c1-8); \
	VOL="kapow-dev-pgdata-$$HASH"; \
	CONT="kapow-dev-$$HASH"; \
	docker rm -f "$$CONT" 2>/dev/null || true; \
	if docker volume inspect "$$VOL" >/dev/null 2>&1; then \
		docker volume rm "$$VOL"; \
		echo "Removed volume $$VOL"; \
	else \
		echo "Volume $$VOL does not exist"; \
	fi

## dev-db-up: Start standalone dev database (PostgreSQL+PostGIS on port 5433, full dataset)
dev-db-up:
	docker compose -f compose/db.yml up -d --wait
	@echo ""
	@echo "Dev database starting on port 5433. Use:"
	@echo "  DATABASE_URL=postgres://postgres:postgres@localhost:5433/kapow?sslmode=disable"

## dev-db-down: Stop and remove the standalone dev database container
dev-db-down:
	docker compose -f compose/db.yml down

## compose-up: Start 3-service production topology (db + api + web) on http://localhost:8080
compose-up:
	docker compose -f compose/compose.yml up -d --build --wait

## compose-down: Stop and remove compose services and volumes
compose-down:
	docker compose -f compose/compose.yml down -v

## compose-logs: Tail logs from all compose services
compose-logs:
	docker compose -f compose/compose.yml logs -f

## dev-prune: Remove kapow-dev volumes older than 30 days
dev-prune:
	@CUTOFF=$$(date -d '30 days ago' +%s 2>/dev/null || date -v-30d +%s); \
	for vol in $$(docker volume ls --filter label=kapow-dev=true -q 2>/dev/null); do \
		CREATED=$$(docker volume inspect "$$vol" --format '{{.CreatedAt}}' 2>/dev/null); \
		VOL_TS=$$(date -d "$$CREATED" +%s 2>/dev/null || date -j -f "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S" "$$CREATED" +%s 2>/dev/null || echo 0); \
		if [ "$$VOL_TS" -lt "$$CUTOFF" ]; then \
			echo "Removing old volume: $$vol (created $$CREATED)"; \
			docker volume rm "$$vol" 2>/dev/null || true; \
		fi; \
	done; \
	echo "Prune complete."

## tools-build: Build all tools (samplesql)
tools-build:
	cd tools/samplesql && go build -o ../../bin/samplesql .

## tools-test: Run tests for all tools
tools-test:
	cd tools/samplesql && go test ./...

## test-quick: Fast checks — Go unit tests, TS type-check + build, ESLint (~10-20s, no DB)
test-quick:
	MAKE=$(MAKE) bash scripts/parallel-run.sh api-test-unit web-build web-lint

## test-standard: Full local validation — quick + web unit tests + Go lint (~30-60s, no DB)
test-standard:
	$(MAKE) test-quick && MAKE=$(MAKE) bash scripts/parallel-run.sh web-test api-lint

## test-fixture: Validate fixture data loads cleanly against current schema (spins up temp Docker PG)
test-fixture:
	bash scripts/test-fixture.sh

## test-full: Complete validation — standard + integration, e2e, smoke, fixture (requires DB + running services)
test-full:
	$(MAKE) test-standard && MAKE=$(MAKE) bash scripts/parallel-run.sh api-test test-fixture tools-test && $(MAKE) web-e2e && $(MAKE) smoke-test

## fixture-sql: Generate complete.sql.xz from JSON fixture data (requires Docker)
fixture-sql:
	bash scripts/gen-fixture-sql.sh

## fixture-json: Re-extract JSON fixture data from a live database (requires DATABASE_URL)
fixture-json:
	cd tools/samplesql && go run . --format json --output-dir ../../common/src/main/resources/data/tables

## spec-gen: Generate reference documentation in docs/specs/generated/
spec-gen:
	bash scripts/gen-specs.sh

## help: List all available targets
help:
	@grep -E '^## ' Makefile | sed 's/## //' | column -t -s ':'

```bash
make compose-down
```

1→# Kapow — College Power Search
     2→
     3→A college/university search platform with a Go API backend and React frontend.
     4→
     5→## Quick Start with Docker (recommended)
     6→
     7→The fastest way to try Kapow — a single Docker image bundles PostgreSQL, the API
     8→server, and the web frontend with pre-loaded fixture data.
     9→
    10→```bash
    11→# Build the all-in-one image
    12→make test-image-build
    13→
    14→# Run it (exposes port 8080)
    15→make test-image-run
    16→```
    17→
    18→Open **http://localhost:8080** in your browser. The container includes:
    19→
    20→- PostgreSQL 15 + PostGIS with pre-loaded college data
    21→- Go API server on internal port 8081
    22→- nginx reverse proxy serving the React frontend and proxying API requests
    23→
    24→No configuration needed — the container handles everything on first boot,
    25→including database initialization and fixture data loading.
    26→
    27→To stop: press `Ctrl+C`.
    28→
    29→---
    30→
    31→## Devcontainer
    32→
    33→See [.devcontainer/README.md](.devcontainer/README.md) for setup instructions
    34→(GitHub Codespaces, VS Code, and CLI).
    35→
    36→---
    37→
    38→## Local Development Setup
    39→
    40→### Prerequisites
    41→
    42→- **Go** 1.23+
    43→- **Node.js** 22+ with **pnpm** 9+ (`npm install -g pnpm`)
    44→- **Docker** (for the dev database container)
    45→- **psql** (PostgreSQL client) — `brew install libpq` on macOS, `apt install postgresql-client` on Linux
    46→
    47→Optional:
    48→
    49→- **golangci-lint** for linting
    50→- **air** for standalone API hot-reload: `go install github.com/air-verse/air@latest` (`make dev` uses `go run` instead, no install needed)
    51→
    52→### Quick start (recommended)
    53→
    54→A single command starts everything — PostgreSQL in Docker, the Go API with hot
    55→reload, and the Vite dev server:
    56→
    57→```bash
    58→make dev
    59→```
    60→
    61→This will:
    62→- Start a PostgreSQL + PostGIS container on port **15432** (won't conflict with a host PostgreSQL)
    63→- Load fixture data on first run (subsequent runs skip this step)
    64→- Run database migrations
    65→- Start the API with **air** (hot reload) on **:8081**
    66→- Start the web dev server on **:8080** with HMR
    67→
    68→Open **http://localhost:8080** and you're ready to go. Press **Ctrl+C** to stop
    69→everything. The database volume persists between runs, so the next `make dev`
    70→starts much faster.
    71→
    72→```bash
    73→make dev-clean    # remove this checkout's DB volume (forces fresh fixture load)
    74→make dev-prune    # remove kapow-dev volumes older than 30 days
    75→```
    76→
    77→### Manual setup (alternative)
    78→
    79→If you prefer to manage PostgreSQL yourself:
    80→
    81→#### 1. Configure environment
    82→
    83→```bash
    84→cp .env.example .env
    85→```
    86→
    87→Edit `.env` and set at minimum:
    88→
    89→```
    90→DATABASE_URL=postgres://user:password@localhost:5432/kapow?sslmode=disable
    91→JWT_SECRET=change-me-to-a-long-random-secret
    92→```
    93→
    94→#### 2. Set up the database
    95→
    96→Create the database, then run migrations:
    97→
    98→```bash
    99→createdb kapow
   100→make api-migrate-up
   101→```
   102→
   103→To load fixture data (from `common/src/main/resources/data/`):
   104→
   105→```bash
   106→xz -dc common/src/main/resources/data/complete.sql.xz | psql kapow
   107→```
   108→
   109→Smaller subsets are available for faster loading:
   110→
   111→- `subset-VT.sql.xz` — Vermont only
   112→- `subset-DE_MA_VT.sql.xz` — Delaware, Massachusetts, Vermont
   113→
   114→#### 3. Start the API
   115→
   116→```bash
   117→make api-run
   118→```
   119→
   120→The API listens on **localhost:8081**. Verify with:
   121→
   122→```bash
   123→curl http://localhost:8081/private/healthcheck | jq .
   124→```
   125→
   126→#### 4. Start the web frontend
   127→
   128→In a separate terminal:
   129→
   130→```bash
   131→make web-install   # first time only
   132→make web-dev
   133→```
   134→
   135→The dev server runs on **localhost:8080** and proxies `/graphql` and `/api`
   136→requests to the API at :8081.
   137→
   138→Open **http://localhost:8080**.
   139→
   140→#### 5. Authentication with Supabase (optional)
   141→
   142→The GraphQL endpoint (`/graphql`) requires a valid JWT. In development you can
   143→use any HS256 secret via `JWT_SECRET`. For production (or to test real login
   144→flows), connect a Supabase project.
   145→
   146→##### Create a Supabase project
   147→
   148→1. Go to [supabase.com/dashboard](https://supabase.com/dashboard) and create a
   149→   new project (or use an existing one).
   150→2. Note your **Project URL** (`https://<project-ref>.supabase.co`) and
   151→   **anon public** key from **Settings → API**.
   152→
   153→##### Configure API environment variables
   154→
   155→In `.env`, set the JWT vars so the Go API can validate Supabase-issued tokens:
   156→
   157→```
   158→JWT_SECRET=<your-supabase-jwt-secret>
   159→JWT_ISSUER=https://<project-ref>.supabase.co/auth/v1
   160→JWT_AUDIENCE=authenticated
   161→```
   162→
   163→The JWT Secret is on **Settings → API → JWT Secret**. Alternatively, paste the
   164→**JWT Public Key** (RS256) into `JWT_PUBLIC_KEY` for asymmetric validation.
   165→
   166→##### Configure frontend environment variables
   167→
   168→The web frontend uses the Supabase JS SDK for login/signup. Add these to `.env`:
   169→
   170→```
   171→VITE_SUPABASE_URL=https://<project-ref>.supabase.co
   172→VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY=<your-anon-key>
   173→```
   174→
   175→The `VITE_` prefix is required so Vite exposes them to browser code.
   176→
   177→##### Enable OAuth providers
   178→
   179→To support social login (Google, Apple, etc.):
   180→
   181→1. In the Supabase dashboard, go to **Authentication → Providers**.
   182→2. **Google**: Enable the Google provider, paste your Google OAuth **Client ID**
   183→   and **Client Secret** (from [Google Cloud Console → Credentials](https://console.cloud.google.com/apis/credentials)),
   184→   and add your Supabase callback URL as an authorized redirect URI in Google.
   185→3. **Apple**: Enable the Apple provider, upload your **Services ID**, **Team ID**,
   186→   **Key ID**, and **private key** (from
   187→   [Apple Developer → Certificates, Identifiers & Profiles](https://developer.apple.com/account/resources)).
   188→   Add the Supabase callback URL to your Apple Sign In configuration.
   189→
   190→Each provider's callback URL is shown in the Supabase dashboard when you enable
   191→it (typically `https://<project-ref>.supabase.co/auth/v1/callback`).
   192→
   193→##### How it works
   194→
   195→On the first authenticated request, the API automatically creates a personal
   196→team and membership for the user (using the JWT `sub` claim as the user ID).
   197→
   198→---
   199→
   200→## Project Structure
   201→
   202→```
   203→kapow/
   204→├── api/          # Go API server (GraphQL, search engine, auth)
   205→├── web/          # React + TypeScript + Vite frontend
   206→├── common/       # Shared resources (generated schema, fixture data)
   207→├── docker/       # Container config (nginx, supervisord, entrypoint)
   208→├── scripts/      # Shell utilities (smoke tests, schema generation)
   209→├── tools/        # Developer utilities (see below)
   210→├── Dockerfile    # All-in-one multi-stage build
   211→└── Makefile      # Root — delegates to api/ and web/
   212→```
   213→
   214→### tools/
   215→
   216→Developer utilities that operate on the database or fixture data.
   217→
   218→| Tool | Description |
   219→|---|---|
   220→| `tools/samplesql` | Deterministic institution sampler — connects to a live database, randomly selects N institutions (default 100, seed 42), extracts all dependent rows, and writes a self-contained SQL fixture file using `COPY` statements. Useful for generating small, reproducible test datasets. |
   221→
   222→**samplesql usage:**
   223→
   224→```bash
   225→cd tools/samplesql
   226→go run . --database-url "$DATABASE_URL" --limit 100 --seed 42 --output sample.sql
   227→```
   228→
   229→Flags: `--database-url` (or `DATABASE_URL` env var), `--limit` (number of institutions), `--seed` (deterministic random seed), `--output` (file path; defaults to stdout).
   230→
   231→---
   232→
   233→## Useful Commands
   234→
   235→### Development
   236→
   237→| Command | Description |
   238→|---|---|
   239→| `make dev` | Start full stack (DB + API + web) with hot reload |
   240→| `make dev-clean` | Remove dev DB volume (fresh fixture load on next run) |
   241→| `make dev-prune` | Remove kapow-dev volumes older than 30 days |
   242→
   243→### API
   244→
   245→| Command | Description |
   246→|---|---|
   247→| `make api-run` | Start the API server |
   248→| `make api-test` | Run all Go tests (with race detector) |
   249→| `make api-test-unit` | Unit tests only (no DB required) |
   250→| `make api-generate` | Regenerate GraphQL code after schema changes |
   251→| `make api-migrate-up` | Apply pending database migrations |
   252→| `make api-migrate-down` | Roll back the last migration |
   253→| `make api-lint` | Run go vet + golangci-lint (includes gosec) |
   254→| `make lint` | Run all linters (Go + web) |
   255→| `make audit` | Security audit on web dependencies |
   256→
   257→### Web
   258→
   259→| Command | Description |
   260→|---|---|
   261→| `make web-dev` | Start Vite dev server with HMR |
   262→| `make web-build` | TypeScript check + production build |
   263→| `make web-test` | Run Vitest unit tests |
   264→| `make web-lint` | ESLint (zero-warning policy) |
   265→| `make web-e2e` | Playwright end-to-end tests |
   266→| `make web-storybook` | Start Storybook on port 6006 |
   267→
   268→### Smoke Tests
   269→
   270→```bash
   271→# Against a running API
   272→bash scripts/smoke-test.sh
   273→
   274→# Builds the API, starts it, tests, and tears down automatically
   275→bash scripts/smoke-test-with-server.sh
   276→```
   277→
   278→---
   279→
   280→## Ports
   281→
   282→| Service | Port |
   283→|---|---|

# Kapow — College Power Search

A college/university search platform with a Go API backend and React frontend.

## Quick Start with Docker (recommended)

The fastest way to try Kapow — a single Docker image bundles PostgreSQL, the API
server, and the web frontend with pre-loaded fixture data.

```bash
# Build the all-in-one image
make test-image-build

# Run it (exposes port 8080)
make test-image-run
```

Open **http://localhost:8080** in your browser. The container includes:

- PostgreSQL 15 + PostGIS with pre-loaded college data
- Go API server on internal port 8081
- nginx reverse proxy serving the React frontend and proxying API requests

No configuration needed — the container handles everything on first boot,
including database initialization and fixture data loading.

To stop: press `Ctrl+C`.

---

## Devcontainer

See [.devcontainer/README.md](.devcontainer/README.md) for setup instructions
(GitHub Codespaces, VS Code, and CLI).

---

## Local Development Setup

### Prerequisites

- **Go** 1.23+
- **Node.js** 22+ with **pnpm** 9+ (`npm install -g pnpm`)
- **Docker** (for the dev database container)
- **psql** (PostgreSQL client) — `brew install libpq` on macOS, `apt install postgresql-client` on Linux

Optional:

- **golangci-lint** for linting
- **air** for standalone API hot-reload: `go install github.com/air-verse/air@latest` (`make dev` uses `go run` instead, no install needed)

### Quick start (recommended)

A single command starts everything — PostgreSQL in Docker, the Go API with hot
reload, and the Vite dev server:

```bash
make dev
```

This will:
- Start a PostgreSQL + PostGIS container on port **15432** (won't conflict with a host PostgreSQL)
- Load fixture data on first run (subsequent runs skip this step)
- Run database migrations
- Start the API with **air** (hot reload) on **:8081**
- Start the web dev server on **:8080** with HMR

Open **http://localhost:8080** and you're ready to go. Press **Ctrl+C** to stop
everything. The database volume persists between runs, so the next `make dev`
starts much faster.

```bash
make dev-clean    # remove this checkout's DB volume (forces fresh fixture load)
make dev-prune    # remove kapow-dev volumes older than 30 days
```

### Manual setup (alternative)

If you prefer to manage PostgreSQL yourself:

#### 1. Configure environment

```bash
cp .env.example .env
```

Edit `.env` and set at minimum:

```
DATABASE_URL=postgres://user:password@localhost:5432/kapow?sslmode=disable
JWT_SECRET=change-me-to-a-long-random-secret
```

#### 2. Set up the database

Create the database, then run migrations:

```bash
createdb kapow
make api-migrate-up
```

To load fixture data (from `common/src/main/resources/data/`):

```bash
xz -dc common/src/main/resources/data/complete.sql.xz | psql kapow
```

Smaller subsets are available for faster loading:

- `subset-VT.sql.xz` — Vermont only
- `subset-DE_MA_VT.sql.xz` — Delaware, Massachusetts, Vermont

#### 3. Start the API

```bash
make api-run
```

The API listens on **localhost:8081**. Verify with:

```bash
curl http://localhost:8081/private/healthcheck | jq .
```

#### 4. Start the web frontend

In a separate terminal:

```bash
make web-install   # first time only
make web-dev
```

The dev server runs on **localhost:8080** and proxies `/graphql` and `/api`
requests to the API at :8081.

Open **http://localhost:8080**.

#### 5. Authentication with Supabase (optional)

The GraphQL endpoint (`/graphql`) requires a valid JWT. In development you can
use any HS256 secret via `JWT_SECRET`. For production (or to test real login
flows), connect a Supabase project.

##### Create a Supabase project

1. Go to [supabase.com/dashboard](https://supabase.com/dashboard) and create a
   new project (or use an existing one).
2. Note your **Project URL** (`https://<project-ref>.supabase.co`) and
   **anon public** key from **Settings → API**.

##### Configure API environment variables

In `.env`, set the JWT vars so the Go API can validate Supabase-issued tokens:

```
JWT_SECRET=<your-supabase-jwt-secret>
JWT_ISSUER=https://<project-ref>.supabase.co/auth/v1
JWT_AUDIENCE=authenticated
```

The JWT Secret is on **Settings → API → JWT Secret**. Alternatively, paste the
**JWT Public Key** (RS256) into `JWT_PUBLIC_KEY` for asymmetric validation.

##### Configure frontend environment variables

The web frontend uses the Supabase JS SDK for login/signup. Add these to `.env`:

```
VITE_SUPABASE_URL=https://<project-ref>.supabase.co
VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY=<your-anon-key>
```

The `VITE_` prefix is required so Vite exposes them to browser code.

##### Enable OAuth providers

To support social login (Google, Apple, etc.):

1. In the Supabase dashboard, go to **Authentication → Providers**.
2. **Google**: Enable the Google provider, paste your Google OAuth **Client ID**
   and **Client Secret** (from [Google Cloud Console → Credentials](https://console.cloud.google.com/apis/credentials)),
   and add your Supabase callback URL as an authorized redirect URI in Google.
3. **Apple**: Enable the Apple provider, upload your **Services ID**, **Team ID**,
   **Key ID**, and **private key** (from
   [Apple Developer → Certificates, Identifiers & Profiles](https://developer.apple.com/account/resources)).
   Add the Supabase callback URL to your Apple Sign In configuration.

Each provider's callback URL is shown in the Supabase dashboard when you enable
it (typically `https://<project-ref>.supabase.co/auth/v1/callback`).

##### How it works

On the first authenticated request, the API automatically creates a personal
team and membership for the user (using the JWT `sub` claim as the user ID).

---

## Project Structure

```
kapow/
├── api/          # Go API server (GraphQL, search engine, auth)
├── web/          # React + TypeScript + Vite frontend
├── common/       # Shared resources (generated schema, fixture data)
├── docker/       # Container config (nginx, supervisord, entrypoint)
├── scripts/      # Shell utilities (smoke tests, schema generation)
├── tools/        # Developer utilities (see below)
├── Dockerfile    # All-in-one multi-stage build
└── Makefile      # Root — delegates to api/ and web/
```

### tools/

Developer utilities that operate on the database or fixture data.

| Tool | Description |
|---|---|
| `tools/samplesql` | Deterministic institution sampler — connects to a live database, randomly selects N institutions (default 100, seed 42), extracts all dependent rows, and writes a self-contained SQL fixture file using `COPY` statements. Useful for generating small, reproducible test datasets. |

**samplesql usage:**

```bash
cd tools/samplesql
go run . --database-url "$DATABASE_URL" --limit 100 --seed 42 --output sample.sql
```

Flags: `--database-url` (or `DATABASE_URL` env var), `--limit` (number of institutions), `--seed` (deterministic random seed), `--output` (file path; defaults to stdout).

---

## Useful Commands

### Development

| Command | Description |
|---|---|
| `make dev` | Start full stack (DB + API + web) with hot reload |
| `make dev-clean` | Remove dev DB volume (fresh fixture load on next run) |
| `make dev-prune` | Remove kapow-dev volumes older than 30 days |

### API

| Command | Description |
|---|---|
| `make api-run` | Start the API server |
| `make api-test` | Run all Go tests (with race detector) |
| `make api-test-unit` | Unit tests only (no DB required) |
| `make api-generate` | Regenerate GraphQL code after schema changes |
| `make api-migrate-up` | Apply pending database migrations |
| `make api-migrate-down` | Roll back the last migration |
| `make api-lint` | Run go vet + golangci-lint (includes gosec) |
| `make lint` | Run all linters (Go + web) |
| `make audit` | Security audit on web dependencies |

### Web

| Command | Description |
|---|---|
| `make web-dev` | Start Vite dev server with HMR |
| `make web-build` | TypeScript check + production build |
| `make web-test` | Run Vitest unit tests |
| `make web-lint` | ESLint (zero-warning policy) |
| `make web-e2e` | Playwright end-to-end tests |
| `make web-storybook` | Start Storybook on port 6006 |

### Smoke Tests

```bash
# Against a running API
bash scripts/smoke-test.sh

# Builds the API, starts it, tests, and tears down automatically
bash scripts/smoke-test-with-server.sh
```

---

## Ports

| Service | Port |
|---|---|
| Web dev server | 8080 |
| API server | 8081 |
| Dev PostgreSQL | 15432 |
| Docker (all-in-one) | 8080 |
| Storybook | 6006 |

---

## Environment Variables

See `.env.example` for the full list. The essential ones:

| Variable | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `DATABASE_URL` | Yes | PostgreSQL connection string |
| `JWT_SECRET` | Yes | Secret for HS256 token signing (or Supabase JWT secret) |
| `JWT_PUBLIC_KEY` | No | RS256 PEM public key — overrides `JWT_SECRET` when set |
| `JWT_ISSUER` | No | Expected `iss` claim (e.g. `https://<ref>.supabase.co/auth/v1`) |
| `JWT_AUDIENCE` | No | Expected `aud` claim (e.g. `authenticated`) |
| `GRAPHQL_PLAYGROUND` | No | Set `true` to enable GraphQL Playground at `/playground` |
| `LOG_LEVEL` | No | `debug`, `info` (default), `warn`, `error` |
| `LOG_FORMAT` | No | `json` (default) or `text` (dev) |
| `CORS_ORIGINS` | No | Allowed origins, e.g. `http://localhost:8080` |

## Kapow Monorepo Makefile — delegates to component Makefiles
## Usage: make <target>

.PHONY: api-build api-run api-generate api-test api-test-unit \
        api-lint api-migrate-up api-migrate-down api-clean schema-gen \
        web-install web-dev web-build web-lint web-clean web-test web-test-coverage web-e2e web-screenshots web-storybook \
        web-schema-sync \
        lint audit hadolint \
        smoke-test smoke-test-with-server \
        api-image-build web-image-build test-image-build test-image-run \
        tools-build tools-test \
        dev dev-clean dev-prune dev-db-up dev-db-down \
        compose-up compose-down compose-logs \
        test-quick test-standard test-full test-fixture \
        fixture-sql fixture-json \
        build-full \
        spec-gen \
        help

## api-build: Build the API server binary
api-build:
	$(MAKE) -C api build

## build-full: Build single binary with embedded web frontend (API + SPA)
build-full: web-build
	$(MAKE) -C api build-full

## api-run: Run the API server
api-run:
	$(MAKE) -C api run

## api-generate: Run GraphQL code generation
api-generate:
	$(MAKE) -C api generate

## api-test: Run all API tests
api-test:
	$(MAKE) -C api test

## api-test-unit: Run API unit tests
api-test-unit:
	$(MAKE) -C api test-unit

## api-lint: Run linter on the API
api-lint:
	$(MAKE) -C api lint

## api-migrate-up: Apply database migrations
api-migrate-up:
	$(MAKE) -C api migrate-up

## api-migrate-down: Roll back last database migration
api-migrate-down:
	$(MAKE) -C api migrate-down

## api-clean: Remove API build artifacts
api-clean:
	$(MAKE) -C api clean

## schema-gen: Generate common/schema/schema.sql from migration Up blocks
schema-gen:
	bash scripts/gen-schema.sh

## web-install: Install web frontend dependencies
web-install:
	$(MAKE) -C web install

## web-dev: Start Vite dev server (port 8080, proxies /graphql → :8081)
web-dev:
	$(MAKE) -C web dev

## web-build: Type-check and build web frontend for production
web-build:
	$(MAKE) -C web build

## web-lint: Run ESLint on web frontend
web-lint:
	$(MAKE) -C web lint

## web-clean: Remove web build artifacts and node_modules
web-clean:
	$(MAKE) -C web clean

## web-test: Run web unit tests
web-test:
	$(MAKE) -C web test

## web-test-coverage: Run web unit tests with coverage report
web-test-coverage:
	$(MAKE) -C web test-coverage

## web-e2e: Run Playwright E2E tests for web frontend (starts Vite dev server automatically)
web-e2e:
	$(MAKE) -C web e2e-test

## web-screenshots: Capture visual baseline screenshots across viewports
web-screenshots:
	$(MAKE) -C web screenshots

## web-storybook: Start Storybook dev server for UI components (port 6006)
web-storybook:
	$(MAKE) -C web storybook

## web-schema-sync: Regenerate web/schema.graphql and graphql-env.d.ts from API SDL files
web-schema-sync:
	cat api/graph/schema/schema.graphql \
	    api/graph/schema/auth.graphql \
	    api/graph/schema/data_sources.graphql \
	    api/graph/schema/echo.graphql \
	    api/graph/schema/field_names_gen.graphql \
	    api/graph/schema/institution.graphql \
	    api/graph/schema/preferences.graphql \
	    api/graph/schema/search.graphql \
	    api/graph/schema/search_fields.graphql \
	    api/graph/schema/similarity.graphql \
	    api/graph/schema/zip.graphql \
	    > web/schema.graphql
	cd web && pnpm exec gql-tada generate-output --tsconfig tsconfig.app.json

## lint: Run all linters (Go + web)
lint: api-lint web-lint

## audit: Run security audit on web dependencies (non-zero exit on high+ severity)
audit:
	cd web && pnpm audit --audit-level=high

## hadolint: Lint all Dockerfiles with hadolint (runs via Docker)
hadolint:
	@find . -name 'Dockerfile*' -not -path '*/node_modules/*' -not -path '*/dist/*' | sort | \
		while read -r f; do \
			echo "Linting $$f"; \
			docker run --rm -i hadolint/hadolint < "$$f" || exit 1; \
		done

## smoke-test: Run search smoke test against localhost:8081 (requires running API)
smoke-test:
	bash scripts/smoke-test.sh

## smoke-test-with-server: Build API, start it, run smoke test, tear down (requires DATABASE_URL)
smoke-test-with-server:
	bash scripts/smoke-test-with-server.sh

## api-image-build: Build the standalone API production Docker image
api-image-build:
	docker build -f api/Dockerfile \
		--build-arg VERSION=$$(git describe --tags --always --dirty 2>/dev/null || echo dev) \
		--build-arg BUILD_TIME=$$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ) \
		-t kapow-api \
		api/

## web-image-build: Build the standalone web frontend Docker image (nginx + Vite build)
web-image-build:
	docker build -t kapow-web web/

## test-image-build: Build the all-in-one test Docker image (postgres+API+nginx)
test-image-build: hadolint
	docker build -t kapow-test .

## test-image-run: Run the test image, exposing the web UI on http://localhost:8080
test-image-run: test-image-build
	docker run --rm -p 8080:80 kapow-test

## dev: Start full-stack dev environment (PostgreSQL + API + web) with hot reload
dev:
	bash scripts/dev.sh

## dev-clean: Remove this checkout's dev database volume (forces fresh fixture load on next `make dev`)
dev-clean:
	@HASH=$$(echo "$$PWD" | md5sum | cut -c1-8); \
	VOL="kapow-dev-pgdata-$$HASH"; \
	CONT="kapow-dev-$$HASH"; \
	docker rm -f "$$CONT" 2>/dev/null || true; \
	if docker volume inspect "$$VOL" >/dev/null 2>&1; then \
		docker volume rm "$$VOL"; \
		echo "Removed volume $$VOL"; \
	else \
		echo "Volume $$VOL does not exist"; \
	fi

## dev-db-up: Start standalone dev database (PostgreSQL+PostGIS on port 5433, full dataset)
dev-db-up:
	docker compose -f compose/db.yml up -d --wait
	@echo ""
	@echo "Dev database starting on port 5433. Use:"
	@echo "  DATABASE_URL=postgres://postgres:postgres@localhost:5433/kapow?sslmode=disable"

## dev-db-down: Stop and remove the standalone dev database container
dev-db-down:
	docker compose -f compose/db.yml down

## compose-up: Start 3-service production topology (db + api + web) on http://localhost:8080
compose-up:
	docker compose -f compose/compose.yml up -d --build --wait

## compose-down: Stop and remove compose services and volumes
compose-down:
	docker compose -f compose/compose.yml down -v

## compose-logs: Tail logs from all compose services
compose-logs:
	docker compose -f compose/compose.yml logs -f

## dev-prune: Remove kapow-dev volumes older than 30 days
dev-prune:
	@CUTOFF=$$(date -d '30 days ago' +%s 2>/dev/null || date -v-30d +%s); \
	for vol in $$(docker volume ls --filter label=kapow-dev=true -q 2>/dev/null); do \
		CREATED=$$(docker volume inspect "$$vol" --format '{{.CreatedAt}}' 2>/dev/null); \
		VOL_TS=$$(date -d "$$CREATED" +%s 2>/dev/null || date -j -f "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S" "$$CREATED" +%s 2>/dev/null || echo 0); \
		if [ "$$VOL_TS" -lt "$$CUTOFF" ]; then \
			echo "Removing old volume: $$vol (created $$CREATED)"; \
			docker volume rm "$$vol" 2>/dev/null || true; \
		fi; \
	done; \
	echo "Prune complete."

## tools-build: Build all tools (samplesql)
tools-build:
	cd tools/samplesql && go build -o ../../bin/samplesql .

## tools-test: Run tests for all tools
tools-test:
	cd tools/samplesql && go test ./...

## test-quick: Fast checks — Go unit tests, TS type-check + build, ESLint (~10-20s, no DB)
test-quick:
	MAKE=$(MAKE) bash scripts/parallel-run.sh api-test-unit web-build web-lint

## test-standard: Full local validation — quick + web unit tests + Go lint (~30-60s, no DB)
test-standard:
	$(MAKE) test-quick && MAKE=$(MAKE) bash scripts/parallel-run.sh web-test api-lint

## test-fixture: Validate fixture data loads cleanly against current schema (spins up temp Docker PG)
test-fixture:
	bash scripts/test-fixture.sh

## test-full: Complete validation — standard + integration, e2e, smoke, fixture (requires DB + running services)
test-full:
	$(MAKE) test-standard && MAKE=$(MAKE) bash scripts/parallel-run.sh api-test test-fixture tools-test && $(MAKE) web-e2e && $(MAKE) smoke-test

## fixture-sql: Generate complete.sql.xz from JSON fixture data (requires Docker)
fixture-sql:
	bash scripts/gen-fixture-sql.sh

## fixture-json: Re-extract JSON fixture data from a live database (requires DATABASE_URL)
fixture-json:
	cd tools/samplesql && go run . --format json --output-dir ../../common/src/main/resources/data/tables

## spec-gen: Generate reference documentation in docs/specs/generated/
spec-gen:
	bash scripts/gen-specs.sh

## help: List all available targets
help:
	@ESSENTIAL="api-generate build-full dev help lint test-full test-quick test-standard web-schema-sync"; \
	echo ""; \
	echo "Usage: make <target>"; \
	echo ""; \
	echo "Essential targets:"; \
	echo ""; \
	grep -E '^## [a-z]' $(MAKEFILE_LIST) | sed 's/## //' | sort -t: -k1,1 | \
		while IFS=: read -r target desc; do \
			for e in $$ESSENTIAL; do \
				if [ "$$target" = "$$e" ]; then \
					printf "  %-24s %s\n" "$$target" "$$desc"; \
					break; \
				fi; \
			done; \
		done; \
	echo ""; \
	echo "All targets:"; \
	echo ""; \
	grep -E '^## [a-z]' $(MAKEFILE_LIST) | sed 's/## //' | sort -t: -k1,1 | \
		while IFS=: read -r target desc; do \
			SKIP=false; \
			for e in $$ESSENTIAL; do \
				if [ "$$target" = "$$e" ]; then SKIP=true; break; fi; \
			done; \
			if [ "$$SKIP" = "false" ]; then \
				printf "  %-24s %s\n" "$$target" "$$desc"; \
			fi; \
		done

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Kapow — College Power Search

A college/university search platform with a Go API backend and React frontend.

## Quick Start with Docker (recommended)

The fastest way to try Kapow — a single Docker image bundles PostgreSQL, the API
server, and the web frontend with pre-loaded fixture data.

```bash
# Build the all-in-one image
make test-image-build

# Run it (exposes port 8080)
make test-image-run
```

Open **http://localhost:8080** in your browser. The container includes:

- PostgreSQL 15 + PostGIS with pre-loaded college data
- Go API server on internal port 8081
- nginx reverse proxy serving the React frontend and proxying API requests

No configuration needed — the container handles everything on first boot,
including database initialization and fixture data loading.

To stop: press `Ctrl+C`.

---

## Devcontainer

See [.devcontainer/README.md](.devcontainer/README.md) for setup instructions
(GitHub Codespaces, VS Code, and CLI).

---

## Local Development Setup

### Prerequisites

- **Go** 1.23+
- **Node.js** 22+ with **pnpm** 9+ (`npm install -g pnpm`)
- **Docker** (for the dev database container)
- **psql** (PostgreSQL client) — `brew install libpq` on macOS, `apt install postgresql-client` on Linux

Optional:

- **golangci-lint** for linting
- **air** for standalone API hot-reload: `go install github.com/air-verse/air@latest` (`make dev` uses `go run` instead, no install needed)

### Quick start (recommended)

A single command starts everything — PostgreSQL in Docker, the Go API with hot
reload, and the Vite dev server:

```bash
make dev
```

This will:
- Start a PostgreSQL + PostGIS container on port **15432** (won't conflict with a host PostgreSQL)
- Load fixture data on first run (subsequent runs skip this step)
- Run database migrations
- Start the API with **air** (hot reload) on **:8081**
- Start the web dev server on **:8080** with HMR

Open **http://localhost:8080** and you're ready to go. Press **Ctrl+C** to stop
everything. The database volume persists between runs, so the next `make dev`
starts much faster.

```bash
make dev-clean    # remove this checkout's DB volume (forces fresh fixture load)
make dev-prune    # remove kapow-dev volumes older than 30 days
```

### Manual setup (alternative)

If you prefer to manage PostgreSQL yourself:

#### 1. Configure environment

```bash
cp .env.example .env
```

Edit `.env` and set at minimum:

```
DATABASE_URL=postgres://user:password@localhost:5432/kapow?sslmode=disable
JWT_SECRET=change-me-to-a-long-random-secret
```

#### 2. Set up the database

Create the database, then run migrations:

```bash
createdb kapow
make api-migrate-up
```

To load fixture data (from `common/src/main/resources/data/`):

```bash
xz -dc common/src/main/resources/data/complete.sql.xz | psql kapow
```

Smaller subsets are available for faster loading:

- `subset-VT.sql.xz` — Vermont only
- `subset-DE_MA_VT.sql.xz` — Delaware, Massachusetts, Vermont

#### 3. Start the API

```bash
make api-run
```

The API listens on **localhost:8081**. Verify with:

```bash
curl http://localhost:8081/private/healthcheck | jq .
```

#### 4. Start the web frontend

In a separate terminal:

```bash
make web-install   # first time only
make web-dev
```

The dev server runs on **localhost:8080** and proxies `/graphql` and `/api`
requests to the API at :8081.

Open **http://localhost:8080**.

#### 5. Authentication with Supabase (optional)

The GraphQL endpoint (`/graphql`) requires a valid JWT. In development you can
use any HS256 secret via `JWT_SECRET`. For production (or to test real login
flows), connect a Supabase project.

##### Create a Supabase project

1. Go to [supabase.com/dashboard](https://supabase.com/dashboard) and create a
   new project (or use an existing one).
2. Note your **Project URL** (`https://<project-ref>.supabase.co`) and
   **anon public** key from **Settings → API**.

##### Configure API environment variables

In `.env`, set the JWT vars so the Go API can validate Supabase-issued tokens:

```
JWT_SECRET=<your-supabase-jwt-secret>
JWT_ISSUER=https://<project-ref>.supabase.co/auth/v1
JWT_AUDIENCE=authenticated
```

The JWT Secret is on **Settings → API → JWT Secret**. Alternatively, paste the
**JWT Public Key** (RS256) into `JWT_PUBLIC_KEY` for asymmetric validation.

##### Configure frontend environment variables

The web frontend uses the Supabase JS SDK for login/signup. Add these to `.env`:

```
VITE_SUPABASE_URL=https://<project-ref>.supabase.co
VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY=<your-anon-key>
```

The `VITE_` prefix is required so Vite exposes them to browser code.

##### Enable OAuth providers

To support social login (Google, Apple, etc.):

1. In the Supabase dashboard, go to **Authentication → Providers**.
2. **Google**: Enable the Google provider, paste your Google OAuth **Client ID**
   and **Client Secret** (from [Google Cloud Console → Credentials](https://console.cloud.google.com/apis/credentials)),
   and add your Supabase callback URL as an authorized redirect URI in Google.
3. **Apple**: Enable the Apple provider, upload your **Services ID**, **Team ID**,
   **Key ID**, and **private key** (from
   [Apple Developer → Certificates, Identifiers & Profiles](https://developer.apple.com/account/resources)).
   Add the Supabase callback URL to your Apple Sign In configuration.

Each provider's callback URL is shown in the Supabase dashboard when you enable
it (typically `https://<project-ref>.supabase.co/auth/v1/callback`).

##### How it works

On the first authenticated request, the API automatically creates a personal
team and membership for the user (using the JWT `sub` claim as the user ID).

---

## Project Structure

```
kapow/
├── api/          # Go API server (GraphQL, search engine, auth)
├── web/          # React + TypeScript + Vite frontend
├── common/       # Shared resources (generated schema, fixture data)
├── docker/       # Container config (nginx, supervisord, entrypoint)
├── scripts/      # Shell utilities (smoke tests, schema generation)
├── tools/        # Developer utilities (see below)
├── Dockerfile    # All-in-one multi-stage build
└── Makefile      # Root — delegates to api/ and web/
```

### tools/

Developer utilities that operate on the database or fixture data.

| Tool | Description |
|---|---|
| `tools/samplesql` | Deterministic institution sampler — connects to a live database, randomly selects N institutions (default 100, seed 42), extracts all dependent rows, and writes a self-contained SQL fixture file using `COPY` statements. Useful for generating small, reproducible test datasets. |

**samplesql usage:**

```bash
cd tools/samplesql
go run . --database-url "$DATABASE_URL" --limit 100 --seed 42 --output sample.sql
```

Flags: `--database-url` (or `DATABASE_URL` env var), `--limit` (number of institutions), `--seed` (deterministic random seed), `--output` (file path; defaults to stdout).

---

## Useful Commands

### Development

| Command | Description |
|---|---|
| `make dev` | Start full stack (DB + API + web) with hot reload |
| `make dev-clean` | Remove dev DB volume (fresh fixture load on next run) |
| `make dev-prune` | Remove kapow-dev volumes older than 30 days |

### API

| Command | Description |
|---|---|
| `make api-run` | Start the API server |
| `make api-test` | Run all Go tests (with race detector) |
| `make api-test-unit` | Unit tests only (no DB required) |
| `make api-generate` | Regenerate GraphQL code after schema changes |
| `make api-migrate-up` | Apply pending database migrations |
| `make api-migrate-down` | Roll back the last migration |
| `make api-lint` | Run go vet + golangci-lint (includes gosec) |
| `make lint` | Run all linters (Go + web) |
| `make audit` | Security audit on web dependencies |

### Web

| Command | Description |
|---|---|
| `make web-dev` | Start Vite dev server with HMR |
| `make web-build` | TypeScript check + production build |
| `make web-test` | Run Vitest unit tests |
| `make web-lint` | ESLint (zero-warning policy) |
| `make web-e2e` | Playwright end-to-end tests |
| `make web-storybook` | Start Storybook on port 6006 |

### Smoke Tests

```bash
# Against a running API
bash scripts/smoke-test.sh

# Builds the API, starts it, tests, and tears down automatically
bash scripts/smoke-test-with-server.sh
```

---

## Ports

| Service | Port |
|---|---|
| Web dev server | 8080 |
| API server | 8081 |
| Dev PostgreSQL | 15432 |
| Docker (all-in-one) | 8080 |
| Storybook | 6006 |

---

## Environment Variables

See `.env.example` for the full list. The essential ones:

| Variable | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `DATABASE_URL` | Yes | PostgreSQL connection string |
| `JWT_SECRET` | Yes | Secret for HS256 token signing (or Supabase JWT secret) |
| `JWT_PUBLIC_KEY` | No | RS256 PEM public key — overrides `JWT_SECRET` when set |
| `JWT_ISSUER` | No | Expected `iss` claim (e.g. `https://<ref>.supabase.co/auth/v1`) |
| `JWT_AUDIENCE` | No | Expected `aud` claim (e.g. `authenticated`) |
| `GRAPHQL_PLAYGROUND` | No | Set `true` to enable GraphQL Playground at `/playground` |
| `LOG_LEVEL` | No | `debug`, `info` (default), `warn`, `error` |
| `LOG_FORMAT` | No | `json` (default) or `text` (dev) |
| `CORS_ORIGINS` | No | Allowed origins, e.g. `http://localhost:8080` |

#!/bin/sh
# entrypoint.sh — first-boot data loading, then supervisord
set -e

PGDATA=/var/lib/postgresql/data
DB_NAME=kapow
DB_USER=postgres
FIXTURE=/data/complete.sql.xz

# ── 1. Initialize PostgreSQL data directory if not already done ───────────────
if [ ! -f "$PGDATA/PG_VERSION" ]; then
    echo "==> Initializing PostgreSQL data directory..."
    su-exec postgres initdb \
        --encoding=UTF8 \
        --locale=C \
        --auth=trust \
        -D "$PGDATA"
    # Ensure postgres listens on TCP for the API and psql commands below
    echo "listen_addresses = 'localhost'" >> "$PGDATA/postgresql.conf"
fi

# ── 2. Start postgres temporarily for first-boot setup ───────────────────────
echo "==> Starting PostgreSQL (init phase)..."
su-exec postgres pg_ctl -D "$PGDATA" -w -t 60 start

# ── 3. Create database if it doesn't exist ───────────────────────────────────
psql -U "$DB_USER" -tc "SELECT 1 FROM pg_database WHERE datname='$DB_NAME'" \
    | grep -q 1 \
    || psql -U "$DB_USER" -c "CREATE DATABASE $DB_NAME"

# ── 4. Load fixture on first boot (sentinel: search_base schema) ─────────────
LOADED=$(psql -U "$DB_USER" -d "$DB_NAME" -tAc \
    "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM pg_namespace WHERE nspname='search_base'" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)

if [ "$LOADED" = "0" ]; then
    echo "==> Loading fixture data — this may take a few minutes..."
    # Filter psql meta-commands (\foo) that aren't the COPY terminator (\.)
    # to match what testdb.loadFixtureSQL does in Go.
    xz -dc "$FIXTURE" \
        | awk '{ if (substr($0,1,1) == "\\" && $0 != "\\.") next; print }' \
        | psql -U "$DB_USER" -d "$DB_NAME" -v ON_ERROR_STOP=0 --no-psqlrc -f -
    echo "==> Fixture loaded."
else
    echo "==> Data already present, skipping fixture import."
fi

# ── 5. Stop the init postgres instance cleanly ───────────────────────────────
echo "==> Stopping init PostgreSQL..."
su-exec postgres pg_ctl -D "$PGDATA" -m fast -w stop

# ── 6. Hand off to supervisord (manages postgres + API + nginx) ───────────────
echo "==> Starting supervisord..."
exec supervisord -n -c /etc/supervisord.conf

file.content:
# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Stage 1: Build the Go API binary
# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
FROM golang:1.24-alpine AS builder-api

ARG VERSION=docker
ARG BUILD_TIME

WORKDIR /build

# Download dependencies first (layer caching)
COPY api/go.mod api/go.sum ./
RUN go mod download

COPY api/ ./

RUN CGO_ENABLED=0 GOOS=linux go build \
        -ldflags "-s -w \
            -X github.com/ketang/kapow/api/internal/version.Version=${VERSION} \
            -X github.com/ketang/kapow/api/internal/version.BuildTime=${BUILD_TIME}" \
        -o /bin/kapow-api \
        ./cmd/server

# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Stage 2: Build the web frontend
# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
FROM node:22-alpine AS builder-web

WORKDIR /build

# Install pnpm then fetch dependencies (layer caching)
RUN npm install -g pnpm@latest

COPY web/package.json web/pnpm-lock.yaml ./
RUN pnpm install --frozen-lockfile

COPY web/ ./
RUN pnpm build

# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Stage 3: Runtime image — postgres+postgis, Go API, nginx, supervisord
# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
FROM postgis/postgis:15-3.4-alpine

# Install runtime deps: nginx (web server + proxy), supervisor (process manager), xz (fixture decompression)
# hadolint ignore=DL3018
RUN apk add --no-cache nginx supervisor xz

# API binary
COPY --from=builder-api /bin/kapow-api /usr/local/bin/kapow-api

# Web assets served by nginx
COPY --from=builder-web /build/dist /var/www/kapow

# Config files
COPY docker/nginx.conf      /etc/nginx/nginx.conf
COPY docker/supervisord.conf /etc/supervisord.conf
COPY docker/entrypoint.sh   /entrypoint.sh

# Fixture data — loaded on first boot
COPY common/src/main/resources/data/complete.sql.xz /data/complete.sql.xz

RUN chmod +x /entrypoint.sh \
    && mkdir -p /run/nginx

EXPOSE 80

ENTRYPOINT ["/entrypoint.sh"]
